2604.23972.title: Quantum Knowledge Graph: Modeling Context-Dependent Triplet Validity
2604.23972.paper_summary: Knowledge graphs (KGs) are increasingly used to support large lan guage model (LLM) reasoning, but standard triplet-based KGs treat each relation as globally valid. In many settings, whether a relation should count as evidence depends on the context. We therefore formulate triplet validity as a triplet-specific function of context and refer to this formulation as a Quantum Knowledge Graph (QKG). We instantiate QKG in medicine using a diabetes-centered PrimeKG subgraph, whose 68,651 context-sensitive relations are further annotated with patient-group-specific constraints. We evaluate it in a reasoner--validator pipeline for medical question answering on a KG-grounded subset of MedReason containing 2,788 questions. With Haiku-4.5 as both the Reasoner and the Validator, KG-backed validation significantly improves over a no-validator baseline ($+0.61$ pp), and QKG with context matching yields the largest gain, outperforming both KG validation without context matching ($+0.79$ pp) and the no-validator baseline ($+1.40$ pp; paired McNemar, all $p<0.05$). Under a stronger validator (Qwen-3.6-Plus), the raw QKG gain over the no-validator baseline grows from $+1.40$ pp to $+5.96$ pp; the context-matching gap is non-significant ($p=0.73$) on the raw set but becomes borderline significant ($p=0.05$) after adjustment for knowledge leakage and suspicious questions, consistent with a benchmark-gold ceiling rather than a QKG limitation. Taken together, the results support the view that the value of a KG in LLM-based clinical reasoning lies not merely in storing medically related facts, but in representing whether those facts are applicable to the specific patient context. For reproducibility and further research, we release the curated QKG datasets and source code.\footnote{https://github.com/HKAI-Sci/QKG}
2604.23972.paper_summary_zh: 知識圖譜（KGs）越來越多地用於支持大型語言模型（LLM）的推理，但標準的基於三元組的KGs將每個關係視為全局有效。在許多情境中，某個關係是否應該被視為證據取決於上下文。因此，我們將三元組的有效性表述為一個特定於三元組的上下文函數，並將這一表述稱為量子知識圖譜（QKG）。我們在醫學中實現QKG，使用以糖尿病為中心的PrimeKG子圖，其68,651個上下文敏感的關係進一步附加了特定於患者群體的約束。我們在一個推理者-驗證者管道中對其進行評估，針對KG基礎的MedReason子集進行醫學問題回答，該子集包含2,788個問題。使用Haiku-4.5作為推理者和驗證者，KG支持的驗證顯著改善了無驗證者基線（$+0.61$ pp），而具有上下文匹配的QKG則產生了最大的增益，超越了沒有上下文匹配的KG驗證（$+0.79$ pp）和無驗證者基線（$+1.40$ pp；配對McNemar，所有$p<0.05$）。在一個更強的驗證者（Qwen-3.6-Plus）下，原始QKG相對於無驗證者基線的增益從$+1.40$ pp增長到$+5.96$ pp；在原始數據集上，上下文匹配的差距不顯著（$p=0.73$），但在調整知識洩露和可疑問題後變得邊際顯著（$p=0.05$），這與基準金標準上限一致，而不是QKG的限制。綜合來看，結果支持這樣的觀點：KG在基於LLM的臨床推理中的價值不僅在於存儲醫學相關事實，還在於表示這些事實是否適用於特定的患者上下文。為了可重複性和進一步研究，我們釋放了經過策劃的QKG數據集和源代碼。\footnote{https://github.com/HKAI-Sci/QKG}
2604.23972.author: Yao Wang et.al.
2604.23972.authors: Yao Wang, Zixu Geng, Jun Yan
